content.content:
-- $Id: testes/pm.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

-- UTF-8 file


print('testing pattern matching')

local function checkerror (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function f (s, p)
  local i,e = string.find(s, p)
  if i then return string.sub(s, i, e) end
end

local a,b = string.find('', '')    -- empty patterns are tricky
assert(a == 1 and b == 0);
a,b = string.find('alo', '')
assert(a == 1 and b == 0)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a', 1)   -- first position
assert(a == 1 and b == 1)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 2)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 5 and b == 7)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 9)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 9 and b == 11)
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', '\0ab', 2);  -- finds at the end
assert(a == 9 and b == 11);
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b')    -- last position
assert(a == 11 and b == 11)
assert(not string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0'))   -- check ending
assert(not string.find('', '\0'))
assert(string.find('alo123alo', '12') == 4)
assert(not string.find('alo123alo', '^12'))

assert(string.match("aaab", ".*b") == "aaab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".*a") == "aaa")
assert(string.match("b", ".*b") == "b")

assert(string.match("aaab", ".+b") == "aaab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".+a") == "aaa")
assert(not string.match("b", ".+b"))

assert(string.match("aaab", ".?b") == "ab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".?a") == "aa")
assert(string.match("b", ".?b") == "b")

assert(f('aloALO', '%l*') == 'alo')
assert(f('aLo_ALO', '%a*') == 'aLo')

assert(f("  \n\r*&\n\r   xuxu  \n\n", "%g%g%g+") == "xuxu")


-- Adapt a pattern to UTF-8
local function PU (p)
  -- reapply '?' into each individual byte of a character.
  -- (For instance, "á?" becomes "\195?\161?".)
  p = string.gsub(p, "(" .. utf8.charpattern .. ")%?", function (c)
    return string.gsub(c, ".", "%0?")
  end)
  -- change '.' to utf-8 character patterns
  p = string.gsub(p, "%.", utf8.charpattern)
  return p
end


assert(f('aaab', 'a*') == 'aaa');
assert(f('aaa', '^.*$') == 'aaa');
assert(f('aaa', 'b*') == '');
assert(f('aaa', 'ab*a') == 'aa')
assert(f('aba', 'ab*a') == 'aba')
assert(f('aaab', 'a+') == 'aaa')
assert(f('aaa', '^.+$') == 'aaa')
assert(not f('aaa', 'b+'))
assert(not f('aaa', 'ab+a'))
assert(f('aba', 'ab+a') == 'aba')
assert(f('a$a', '.$') == 'a')
assert(f('a$a', '.%$') == 'a$')
assert(f('a$a', '.$.') == 'a$a')
assert(not f('a$a', '$$'))
assert(not f('a$b', 'a$'))
assert(f('a$a', '$') == '')
assert(f('', 'b*') == '')
assert(not f('aaa', 'bb*'))
assert(f('aaab', 'a-') == '')
assert(f('aaa', '^.-$') == 'aaa')
assert(f('aabaaabaaabaaaba', 'b.*b') == 'baaabaaabaaab')
assert(f('aabaaabaaabaaaba', 'b.-b') == 'baaab')
assert(f('alo xo', '.o$') == 'xo')
assert(f(' \n isto é assim', '%S%S*') == 'isto')
assert(f(' \n isto é assim', '%S*$') == 'assim')
assert(f(' \n isto é assim', '[a-z]*$') == 'assim')
assert(f('um caracter ? extra', '[^%sa-z]') == '?')
assert(f('', 'a?') == '')
assert(f('á', PU'á?') == 'á')
assert(f('ábl', PU'á?b?l?') == 'ábl')
assert(f('  ábl', PU'á?b?l?') == '')
assert(f('aa', '^aa?a?a') == 'aa')
assert(f(']]]áb', '[^]]+') == 'áb')
assert(f("0alo alo", "%x*") == "0a")
assert(f("alo alo", "%C+") == "alo alo")
print('+')


local function f1 (s, p)
  p = string.gsub(p, "%%([0-9])", function (s)
        return "%" .. (tonumber(s)+1)
       end)
  p = string.gsub(p, "^(^?)", "%1()", 1)
  p = string.gsub(p, "($?)$", "()%1", 1)
  local t = {string.match(s, p)}
  return string.sub(s, t[1], t[#t] - 1)
end

assert(f1('alo alx 123 b\0o b\0o', '(..*) %1') == "b\0o b\0o")
assert(f1('axz123= 4= 4 34', '(.+)=(.*)=%2 %1') == '3= 4= 4 3')
assert(f1('=======', '^(=*)=%1$') == '=======')
assert(not string.match('==========', '^([=]*)=%1$'))

local function range (i, j)
  if i <= j then
    return i, range(i+1, j)
  end
end

local abc = string.char(range(0, 127)) .. string.char(range(128, 255));

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole

# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

cd "$ROOT"

OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

content.text: I see the bug. The test failing is `assert(f(']]]áb', '[^]]+') == 'áb')`. Let me check the `classend` function for the `[^]]` pattern (negated class containing `]`).

delta.text: Found it. The `classend` function bug: when parsing `[

^]]`, it returns position 3 (after first `]`) instead of position 4 (after the second `]`). 

The C re
ference uses a `do-while` loop that unconditionally consumes the first character after `[` (or `[^`), then chec
ks if the *current* position is `]` to terminate. This means a `]` immediately after `[` or `[^` is treated as a literal class member, not a ter

minator. The Rust port instead checks-then-consumes, terminating on the first `]` it sees.

This makes `[
^]]+` parse with `ec=2` instead of `ec=3` in `matchbrac
ketclass`, so the loop body never runs and **any** character matches `[^]]`, including `]`

— so the match swallows the entire `]]]áb` string.

Let me apply the fix:

content.text:
Found it. The `classend` function bug: when parsing `[^]]`, it returns position 3 (after first `]`) instead of position 4 (after the second `]`). 

The C reference uses a `do-while` loop that unconditionally consumes the first character after `[` (or `[^`), then checks if the *current* position is `]` to terminate. This means a `]` immediately after `[` or `[^` is treated as a literal class member, not a terminator. The Rust port instead checks-then-consumes, terminating on the first `]` it sees.

This makes `[^]]+` parse with `ec=2` instead of `ec=3` in `matchbracketclass`, so the loop body never runs and **any** character matches `[^]]`, including `]` — so the match swallows the entire `]]]áb` string.

Let me apply the fix:

delta.text: Build

is clean (only pre-existing warnings). Per instructions, I won't re-run the test.

## Report

**Root cause:
** `classend` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:573
-592` parsed the character class `[^]]` (negated
class containing literal `]`) incorrectly. The C reference uses a `do-while` that unconditionally consumes the first character after `[` or `[^`
before checking for `]` as terminator. The Rust port instead checked-then-consumed, so it tre
ated the very first `]` after `[^` as the class terminator — returning end-position 3 instead of 4
for `[^]]`. This caused `matchbracketclass` to receive `ec=2` (empty range), so the neg
ated class matched every byte — including `]` — and `f(']]]á

b', '[^]]+')` returned `']]]áb'` instead of `'áb'`.

**Fix:** Rest
ructured the loop body to read the current char (treating `]` as literal class content on the first iteration), then check
the *next* position for `]` as the terminator — matching the C `do-while` semantics. Verified traces against `[^]]`, `[]

]`, `[a-z]`, `[%a]`, `[a` (malformed), and `[%` (malformed).

**New state:** Did
not re-run the test per instructions; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. The mega-loop will re-test.

content.text:
Build is clean (only pre-existing warnings). Per instructions, I won't re-run the test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `classend` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:573-592` parsed the character class `[^]]` (negated class containing literal `]`) incorrectly. The C reference uses a `do-while` that unconditionally consumes the first character after `[` or `[^` before checking for `]` as terminator. The Rust port instead checked-then-consumed, so it treated the very first `]` after `[^` as the class terminator — returning end-position 3 instead of 4 for `[^]]`. This caused `matchbracketclass` to receive `ec=2` (empty range), so the negated class matched every byte — including `]` — and `f(']]]áb', '[^]]+')` returned `']]]áb'` instead of `'áb'`.

**Fix:** Restructured the loop body to read the current char (treating `]` as literal class content on the first iteration), then check the *next* position for `]` as the terminator — matching the C `do-while` semantics. Verified traces against `[^]]`, `[]]`, `[a-z]`, `[%a]`, `[a` (malformed), and `[%` (malformed).

**New state:** Did not re-run the test per instructions; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. The mega-loop will re-test.
